stem: A decision-maker must choose among 4 project methodology options. The probability of favorable team experience and project size is uncertain, estimated between 44% and 64%. The payoffs (in $K) for each option are: Agile: $112K in good team experience and project size, $60K in bad team experience and project size. Waterfall: $240K in good team experience and project size, $132K in bad team experience and project size. Hybrid: $122K in good team experience and project size, $66K in bad team experience and project size. Kanban: $256K in good team experience and project size, $84K in bad team experience and project size. Which option maximizes expected value? Does the optimal choice change across the range of probability estimates? What is the expected value of the best option?

ground_truth.answer: At P(good)=0.54: best option is Waterfall with EV=$190.3K. Waterfall is optimal across the entire uncertainty range. EV range of best option: [179.5, 201.1].

solution_steps: Compute EV for each option: EV = P(good)×payoff_good + P(bad)×payoff_bad.
Waterfall is optimal across the entire uncertainty range.
common_failure_modes: Evaluating options only at the midpoint probability without sensitivity analysis
Not identifying the breakeven probability where the optimal choice switches
Ignoring downside risk and focusing only on expected value
